label: Large language models

node.label: Large language models
node.definition: Large language models are high-capacity sequence models pretrained to predict or reconstruct language and adapted for generation, reasoning, retrieval, and tool use.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Large language models is modeled as depending on Decoder-only causal transformers, Large-scale pretraining and feeding downstream nodes such as Conversational assistants & chatbots, Code models, copilots & repository intelligence, Automated theorem proving & mathematical AI.
examples: atlas downstream link to Conversational assistants & chatbots
atlas downstream link to Code models, copilots & repository intelligence
atlas downstream link to Automated theorem proving & mathematical AI
limitations: End-to-end quality is bounded by the weakest data, model, tool, interface, policy, infrastructure, and human-oversight component. Its direct dependencies include Decoder-only causal transformers, Large-scale pretraining.
